title: Interactive toolkit (KM generator + HR-under-non-PH simulator) on open/synthetic data
context: The stats-for-clinicians explainers are enhanced by showing, not just telling — a clinician who can manipulate a KM curve generator to see what happens when censoring increases, or who can see how a hazard ratio changes (or becomes misleading) under crossing survival curves, gains intuition that prose alone cannot convey. The toolkit is small, MIT-licensed, runs entirely on open/synthetic data (no patient data, no clinical calculation on a user's own data), and every widget carries the 'educational, not clinical advice' frame. It depends on repro-005 (reproducibility harness) and the pilot explainers (hr-004, km-006) for the concept validation.
objective: Build and merge an MIT-licensed interactive toolkit with at minimum a Kaplan-Meier curve generator and an HR-under-non-proportional-hazards simulator, both running on open/synthetic data, with deterministic tested figures, accessibility, and the 'not clinical advice' frame on every widget.
acceptanceCriteria: The KM-curve generator allows users to vary parameters (e.g. sample size, event rate, censoring fraction, number of groups) and renders a Kaplan-Meier curve with at-risk table and confidence intervals; all computation is on synthetic data generated deterministically from a committed seed.

The HR-under-non-proportional-hazards simulator allows users to see how the estimated HR changes as crossing time varies, illustrating that the HR is a misleading single-number summary when hazards cross; all computation is on synthetic data.
Neither widget performs clinical calculation on a user's own patient data; both run only on open/synthetic data generated within the tool.
Each widget carries a visible structural 'educational, not clinical advice' frame and links its source claims to the relevant explainer.

Figures are deterministic (same seed produces identical output), tested (golden output checksummed in CI), accessible (alt-text, colorblind-safe palettes), and reproducible under the repro-005 harness.
The toolkit is MIT-licensed; CI is green; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; no embedded credentials or secrets.
At least one integration test demonstrates the full generate-figure pipeline from input parameters to checksummed output.
resources: tasks/stats-for-clinicians-hr-004.json — HR and proportional-hazards concepts (prerequisite for simulator)
tasks/stats-for-clinicians-km-006.json — KM and censoring concepts (prerequisite for generator)
PLAN.md — Solution approach (figure/interactive toolkit); Non-goals (no clinical calculation on user data)
Open statistical computation methods for KM estimation and Cox model; colorblind-safe palette libraries
output: A merged MIT-licensed interactive toolkit PR containing a KM-curve generator and an HR-under-non-PH simulator, both running on open/synthetic data, with deterministic tested figures, accessibility, CI green, and the 'not clinical advice' frame on every widget.
